strengths: 19+ years of hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems (e.g., micro-services handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform deployed on Kubernetes and used by Green Map
Current exposure to AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling
concerns: No director/principal-level scope in career history — roles are senior/staff IC on delivery teams, not organization-wide technical strategy or leadership
AI experience is recent (since April 2025) and tooling-oriented; no recognized AI expertise, shipped AI products, or advanced degree in an AI-related field
Limited evidence of advising strategic customers/partners or driving cloud platform strategy; cloud experience is application-level (AWS CDK, k8s) rather than platform vision

key_factors.factor: AI strategy expertise and recognized AI products
15+ years engineering experience with founder/entrepreneurial background

response.justification: The candidate clears the minimum bar: a Computer Science BS and roughly 19 years of engineering experience spanning low-latency systems (DLang micro-services at Dunnhumby), full-stack product work, and DevOps. The founder role at GISCollective evidences the preferred entrepreneurial trait, and current MCP/agentic-tooling work at re:cinq shows genuine but shallow AI exposure. However, the role demands recognized AI expertise with demonstrated AI-built products, cloud platform strategy, and director-level influence — the résumé shows none of these: AI work spans barely a year, there are no AI products or publications, and no evidence of advising customers or setting technical strategy beyond team scope. Strong senior/staff engineer, but a poor level and domain match for a principal AI-strategy position.
